entities.name: SORGHUM
entities.description: The crop studied in the field trial to evaluate the effects of plant population on growth parameters.

entities.name: PATANCHERU, ANDHRA PRADESH, INDIA
entities.description: The location where the field trial was conducted.

entities.name: RAINY SEASON
entities.description: The environmental condition and season during which the trial took place.

entities.name: RADIATION INTERCEPTION
entities.description: The measurement of photosynthetically active radiation intercepted by the crop canopy.

entities.name: LEAF AREA INDEX
entities.description: A measure of the leaf area per unit ground area used to assess crop growth.

entities.name: TOTAL BIOMASS
entities.description: The total dry matter of the crop used as an indicator of growth.

entities.name: PLANT POPULATION
entities.description: The density of plants sown per unit area, tested at 2.5, 5.0, 10.0, and 20.0 plants/m2.

entities.name: CEPTOMETER MEASUREMENTS
entities.description: The use of a ceptometer to measure light interception for estimating crop growth.

entities.name: CROP GROWTH
entities.description: The overall process of plant development and biomass accumulation.

relationships.description: Used as a tool to document plant population effects in sorghum
Higher plant populations resulted in higher leaf area index
Higher plant populations resulted in higher total biomass
Radiation interception is used to predict leaf area
Radiation interception is used to predict total biomass
Ceptometer measurements are used to quantify light/radiation interception
Used as an inexpensive way of estimating crop growth differences
The sorghum field trial was conducted at this location
The sorghum field trial was conducted during this season
